sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_table (id INT PRIMARY KEY, value FLOAT, description VARCHAR(255));
INSERT INTO test_table (id, value, description) VALUES (1, 0.0, 'Value at the boundary of log'), (2, 1.0, 'Log of 1'), (3, 10.0, 'Positive value'), (4, -1.0, 'Negative value for log');
SELECT id, log(value) AS log_value FROM test_table WHERE value NOT BETWEEN -1 AND 0;
SELECT id, log(value) AS log_value FROM test_table WHERE value NOT BETWEEN (SELECT MIN(value) FROM test_table WHERE description LIKE '%Value%') AND (SELECT MAX(value) FROM test_table);
SELECT id, CONCAT('The LOG of ', value, ' is: ', CAST(log(value) AS VARCHAR)) AS log_description FROM test_table WHERE value NOT BETWEEN 5 AND 15 AND value IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, log(MAX(value)) AS max_log_value FROM test_table GROUP BY id HAVING max_log_value NOT BETWEEN -1 AND 0;
CREATE TABLE extra_table (id INT PRIMARY KEY, ref_id INT, description VARCHAR(255), FOREIGN KEY (ref_id) REFERENCES test_table(id));
INSERT INTO extra_table (id, ref_id, description) VALUES (1, 1, 'Reference to a log boundary'), (2, 2, 'Reference to log of 1');
SELECT et.id, (log(tt.value) - log(1)) AS adjusted_log FROM extra_table et JOIN test_table tt ON et.ref_id = tt.id WHERE tt.value NOT BETWEEN 0 AND 10;
WITH log_values AS (SELECT id, log(value) AS log_val FROM test_table WHERE value IS NOT NULL) SELECT AVG(log_val) AS avg_log FROM log_values WHERE log_val NOT BETWEEN -3 AND 0;
